purpose: Primary default-tuning window for durable baseline ranking and planner behavior.
why_this_first: It is the least distorted of the three benchmark windows.
It is best for calibrating durable default weights before stress testing holiday/festival and long-weekend behavior.
It provides the reference baseline against which S2_FESTIVAL_STRESS and S3_LONG_WEEKEND should be judged.

core: World, India, Business, and Technology should show strong top-10 relevance.
Freshness should matter visibly without over-penalizing important continuing stories.
Duplicate and section-bleed leakage should stay low.
Source quality should matter, but no single source should dominate solely because of weight inflation.
anti_patterns: Same-event duplicates occupying multiple top slots.
Entertainment or social spillover into serious sections without gold support.
Weak local stories outranking stronger world/business/technology stories without clear section rationale.

core: Movies, events, and alerts should be detected with good precision.
Date extraction should be correct or within the allowed bucket for weekly planning.
The weekly plan should include useful, future-looking, actionable items rather than article noise.
anti_patterns: Reviews, recaps, box office chatter, and gossip included as planner positives.
Past-only items included without still-actionable value.
Weekly plan overfilled with weak or repetitive items.

execution_order: Draft gold labels for each news section.
Draft gold labels for each Up Ahead category.
Run the app logic on the frozen inputs.
Recommend no more than 5 bounded config changes.
next_after_this: Create S2_FESTIVAL_STRESS.v2.strict.json after S1 gold is stable.
Use S2 only after S1 baseline is working, so holiday/festival stress is compared against a clean reference.
Create S3_LONG_WEEKEND.v2.strict.json after S2, to validate temporal and planner robustness.
